summary.intent: Fix and resolve new PR review comments after a merge conflict resolution, specifically addressing findings from a Greptile automated review.
summary.outcome: Removed a duplicate HashMap key in validate.rs (merge artifact at lines 396/399), determined the tracing feature gate finding was not actionable for this PR, then committed and pushed the fix.

repo: moltis-agents crate uses tracing as an unconditional dependency throughout runner.rs and model.rs — no feature gate convention in this crate, unlike some others in the monorepo.
CLAUDE.md specifies that tracing should be feature-gated, but this rule is inconsistently applied across crates.
Greptile is used as an automated PR reviewer and produces P1/P2 prioritized findings.

code.finding: Duplicate HashMap key insertion — a merge artifact left two identical entries. Only one should remain.

workflow: Greptile PR summaries can flag pre-existing convention gaps as findings; evaluate each against crate-local patterns before acting.
After resolving merge conflicts, automated reviewers may surface duplicate code that the auto-merge introduced.

friction: Initial PR comment fetch returned an already-resolved/minimized comment, requiring a second fetch for the latest Greptile summary.
Merge conflict resolution left a duplicate HashMap key that wasn't caught until the automated review.
open_items: Tracing feature gate inconsistency in moltis-agents crate — CLAUDE.md mandates it but the crate uses tracing unconditionally throughout; this is a pre-existing tech debt not addressed in this PR.
